content: A mutant plant is unable to produce materials or precursors that form Casparian strip. This plant would be

options.content: unable to transport water from roots to the leaves
able to exert greater root pressure than the normal plant
unable to transport food from leaves to roots
unable to control amount of water and solute it absorbs

explanation: Presence of Casparian strips in plants controls the amount of water and solute it absorbs. Thus, a plant that cannot produce Casparian strips effectively will be unable to control the amount of water and solute in plants.

content:
Cell A has osmotic pressure of $$-$$20 bars and pressure potential of 5 bars, whereas cell B has osmotic pressure of $$-$$18 bars and pressure potential of 2 bars.

The direction of flow of water will be

options.content: from cell B to cell A

explanation:
The water potential is the chemical potential of the water, which is equivalent to diffusion pressure deficit with negative sign. Therefore, water potential $$\left(\Psi_A\right)$$ of cell A is

$$\begin{gathered} \Psi_A=\Psi_S+\Psi_P=-20+5=-15 \text { bars } \\ \Psi_B=\Psi_S+\Psi_P=-18+2=-2 \text { bars } \end{gathered}$$

Since, water moves from higher water potential to lower potential, the flow of water will be from cell B ($$-$$2 bars) to cell A ($$-$$15 bars).

content: Which of the following pathways occurs through cell wall?
explanation: In apoplast pathway, water passes from root hairs to xylem through cell wall only without crossing any membranes. The apoplast involves a continuous system of cell walls and intercellular air spaces in plant tissues.
